summary.intent: Implement Matrix OIDC authentication (Issue #711) so users on homeservers using Matrix Authentication Service (MAS/MSC3861) can connect to Moltis, which previously only supported password and access_token auth modes.
summary.outcome: Full OIDC two-phase browser flow implemented across Rust backend and JS frontend, PR #730 created and merged clean after 4 Greptile review iterations resolving 9 comments, plus a critical post-merge bug fix for OIDC trait dispatch.

repo: ChannelPlugin trait methods must be overridden in `impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin` — inherent `impl MatrixPlugin` methods are invisible when calling through `&dyn ChannelPlugin` in the gateway registry
Gateway calls plugins via `Arc<RwLock<dyn ChannelPlugin>>`, so any new plugin capability must be added as a trait method with a default noop, not an inherent method
matrix-sdk 0.16 re-exports `matrix_sdk::store::RoomLoadSettings` from `matrix_sdk_base::store` — use the re-exported path to avoid adding matrix-sdk-base as an explicit dep
RPC response data is accessed via `res.payload`, not `res.result` — a subtle naming difference that caused silent failures in OIDC auth URL extraction
Error conversion from `moltis_channels::Error` to `ServiceError` in gateway uses `.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?`

The `oidc_issuer` config field was removed because matrix-sdk 0.16 discovers the issuer automatically from the homeserver URL with no API to override it — keeping dead config was worse than removing it
Session files with tokens should use `0o600` permissions via `tokio::fs::OpenOptions` + `OpenOptionsExt::mode()` on unix

code.finding: OIDC methods must live in `impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin`, not in `impl MatrixPlugin`, to be callable through `dyn ChannelPlugin` in the gateway
PersistedOidcSession must NOT use #[derive(Debug)] — tokens would leak; use a manual Debug impl with [REDACTED] for sensitive fields
Redirect URI construction for OIDC callback must use url::Url builder with query_pairs_mut().append_pair() to prevent URL parameter injection
setInterval IDs for polling must be stored in a useRef and cleared in resetForm() and modal onClose to prevent interval leaks on unmount

workflow: Greptile code review runs automatically on push; 4 iterations were needed to reach 0 comments — stale comments from old commits often reappear on new commits at shifted line numbers
When resolving merge conflicts between two test modules in the same file, both sets of tests should be kept (union, not one-wins)
biome check --write should be run on all modified JS files as part of the quality gate before committing
cargo clippy on modified crates specifically (not workspace-wide) avoids noise from pre-existing issues in unrelated crates like moltis-providers

friction: Inherent methods on MatrixPlugin were silently ignored when called through dyn ChannelPlugin — caused a runtime error 'OIDC login not supported' that only surfaced post-merge during manual QA, not in compilation or tests
Localized::new and Raw::new type inference failures required explicit type annotations that weren't obvious from the matrix-sdk docs
Clippy false positive on `#[cfg(unix)]` unix import in async file permission code required restructuring with cfg_if-style inline cfg blocks
res.result vs res.payload naming caused two separate bugs (auth_url and channel list) that were only caught by Greptile review, not by any tests
Rate limit hits mid-session required the user to say 'proceed' twice to continue, interrupting implementation flow
Accidental edit corruption in plugin.rs during the inherent→trait method migration required careful recovery by reading surrounding context
open_items: E2E Playwright tests (channels-matrix.spec.js) were listed in the plan but not implemented — the plan called for them but the implementation transcript ends without creating this file
oidc_issuer config field was removed entirely; if matrix-sdk ever exposes an API to override the OIDC issuer URL, it should be re-added
Token refresh persistence via spawn_session_persistence_task uses a background task — no mechanism to detect if the task dies silently (no health check or restart)
Cross-signing bootstrap for OIDC uses bootstrap_cross_signing_if_needed(None) — the OAuth reset flow (CrossSigningResetAuthType::OAuth) path should be verified with a real MAS homeserver
The onboarding MatrixForm OIDC flow was implemented but no polling cleanup was verified for the case where the user navigates away from onboarding mid-flow
